title: Audit & fix elementary-data 0.23.3 PyPI supply-chain compromise
description:
Security advisory (2026-04-27): elementary-data 0.23.3 on PyPI was compromised with a secrets-stealing payload (SSH keys, cloud credentials, crypto wallets, CI secrets). Safe replacement is 0.23.4.

Source: https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/pypi-package-with-11m-monthly-downloads-hacked-to-push-infostealer/

Required actions:

1. Audit this project for any reference to `elementary-data`:
   - pyproject.toml (any [project], [tool.poetry], [project.optional-dependencies], [dependency-groups])
   - requirements*.txt files
   - Lock files: uv.lock, poetry.lock, Pipfile.lock, requirements.lock
   - Transitive dependencies in lock files (search for `elementary-data` or `elementary_data` in any lock file)

2. If `elementary-data` is NOT referenced anywhere (direct or transitive): no code changes are needed. Produce a handoff stating "not affected" and exit successfully. Do not edit any files.

3. If `elementary-data` IS referenced:
   a. Update the dependency declaration in pyproject.toml (or requirements*.txt) to pin to `elementary-data>=0.23.4` (explicitly exclude 0.23.3 — using `>=0.23.4` is sufficient since 0.23.4 is the safe replacement).
   b. Regenerate the lock file using the project's standard tooling (`uv lock`, `poetry lock`, `pip-compile`, etc. — match what the project already uses).
   c. Verify the resulting lock file contains no reference to 0.23.3 anywhere (direct or transitive).
   d. Run the full test suite (`uv run pytest tests/ -n auto -q` if applicable, otherwise the project's standard test command) to confirm nothing broke.

4. Report in the handoff:
   - Whether `elementary-data` was found (yes/no, and if yes: direct or transitive)
   - Whether version 0.23.3 was ever resolved in the lock file (yes/no — this matters for downstream secret-rotation decisions by the operator)
   - The exact action taken (file paths modified, new version pin, lock file regenerated)
   - Test results

Do NOT rotate any secrets — that is an operator-level decision outside the scope of this bead. Only report whether 0.23.3 was historically resolved so the operator can decide.

Acceptance criteria:
- Lock file (if any) contains no reference to elementary-data 0.23.3
- pyproject.toml or requirements files (if they referenced the package) are pinned to >=0.23.4
- Tests pass
- Handoff clearly states findings: not affected / fixed / historically exposed

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `Python` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `python -m py_compile`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `pytest` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
